c74object.name: tap.pitchaccum~
c74object.digest: Dual pitch transposer with accumulating feedback and preset morphing
c74object.description: The tap.pitchaccum~ object is a recreation of the GRM Tools Classic "PitchAccum" plugin: two independent granular transposers ("shadows"), each shifting the input up or down by up to two octaves, with its own delay of up to three seconds, feedback, and gain. The feedback re-enters the transposer, so every pass through the loop is transposed again — the pitch accumulates, spiraling upward or downward pass after pass. A global LFO modulates the transposition of both voices (voice 2 at a settable phase offset) and independent random generators add aperiodic motion. The window and xfade attributes expose the grain engine (a phasor sweeping two crossfaded taps, as in tap.shift~, with a constant-amplitude complementary envelope pair). An optional pitch follower adapts the grain window to the detected period of the input. Every parameter is interpolated per sample, and sixteen preset slots can be stored and morphed between over a settable interpolation time. The object is single-channel; wrap it in mc. for multichannel (the original plugin's stereo-width control is intentionally omitted).

method.name: store
method.description: Store the current parameters in a preset slot (1-16).

method.name: recall
method.description: Morph all parameters from their current values to a stored preset (1-16). The optional second argument overrides the morph time in milliseconds; without it the interp attribute is used. Moving an individual parameter during a morph overrides the morph for that parameter only.

method.name: clear
method.description: Clear the delay buffers and feedback state of both transposers.

attribute.name: trans1
attribute.description: Transposition of voice 1 in semitones (-24..24, fractional values allowed). Likewise trans2.

attribute.name: trans2
attribute.description: Transposition of voice 2 in semitones (-24..24).

attribute.name: delay1
attribute.description: Delay of voice 1 in milliseconds (0..3000). Likewise delay2.

attribute.name: delay2
attribute.description: Delay of voice 2 in milliseconds (0..3000).

attribute.name: fb1
attribute.description: Feedback of voice 1 (0..99). The fed-back signal re-enters the transposer, so each pass is transposed again and the pitch accumulates. Likewise fb2.

attribute.name: fb2
attribute.description: Feedback of voice 2 (0..99).

attribute.name: gain1
attribute.description: Gain of voice 1 (0..100, linear). Likewise gain2.

attribute.name: gain2
attribute.description: Gain of voice 2 (0..100, linear).

attribute.name: window
attribute.description: Grain window in milliseconds (5..200). Small windows smear transients less but add more modulation artifact; large windows are smoother but blur attacks. When the pitch follower is enabled it overrides this with a window derived from the detected period of the input.

attribute.name: xfade
attribute.description: Crossfade between the two grain taps as a percentage (1..100). 100 is a full raised-cosine overlap; smaller values give longer single-tap plateaus. The envelope pair sums to exactly one, so the output level is constant at any setting.

attribute.name: modfreq
attribute.description: Periodic (LFO) modulation frequency in Hz (0..20).

attribute.name: moddepth
attribute.description: Periodic modulation depth in semitones (0..24).

attribute.name: modphase
attribute.description: LFO phase offset between the two voices in degrees (0..360).

attribute.name: randdepth
attribute.description: Random modulation depth in semitones (0..24). Each voice has an independent generator.

attribute.name: randrate
attribute.description: Random modulation rate in Hz (0..50).

attribute.name: follow
attribute.description: Enable the pitch follower: the grain window adapts to roughly two periods of the detected input pitch (autocorrelation, 50-800 Hz search range) and falls back to the window attribute when the input is unpitched. Off by default.

attribute.name: mix
attribute.description: Dry/wet crossfade (0 = dry, 100 = wet), equal-power.

attribute.name: gain
attribute.description: Output gain in dB.

attribute.name: interp
attribute.description: Default preset-morph time in milliseconds used by the recall message.

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Anti-zipper interpolation time in milliseconds applied to direct parameter changes.

attribute.name: bypass
attribute.description: Pass the input through unprocessed.

attribute.name: mute
attribute.description: Silence the output.
